stories.title: Opinion | A New US-Israel Defence Bill Has Opened An Unexpected Door For India
stories.summary: The United States House of Representatives has approved the Fiscal Year 2027 National Defense Authorization Act by a 216-212 vote, a crucial step towards integrating the US and Israeli defence innovation ecosystems. This move is expected to have significant implications for India, one of New Delhi's closest defence partners, as it may alter the dynamics of their partnership. The bill must now pass the Senate before it can be sent to the President for signature.

stories.title: New NFL Rumors on Joe Mixon's Future After Missing Entire 2025 Season With Foot Injury
stories.summary: Former Cincinnati Bengals and Houston Texans running back Joe Mixon has announced his retirement from the NFL, citing a lingering foot injury that has required multiple surgeries. Mixon missed the entire 2025 season due to the injury and was released by the Texans in March. His decision comes after a successful career, including four 1,000-yard rushing seasons and two Pro Bowl nods.

stories.title: Hosepipe ban in force as drought threatens - is your area affected?
stories.summary: A hosepipe ban is now in effect for over 20 million people in England and Wales due to severe drought conditions, with North Wales and the Upper Severn river basin declared drought areas. This ban restricts the use of hosepipes for watering gardens, washing vehicles and windows, and filling pools. The UK is experiencing its driest July on record since 190 years ago, with no significant rainfall expected in the coming days.

stories.title: Oil prices hit $100 for the first time since May
stories.summary: Oil prices have surpassed $100 a barrel for the first time since May, driven by a surge in Brent crude of over 6% following US military strikes against Iran and a Houthi militia attack on oil tankers in the Red Sea. This escalation in the Middle East conflict has reignited fears over global energy supplies, with gas prices also rising steadily. The ongoing conflict risks pushing up inflation in countries like the UK and the US, leading to higher prices for consumers.

stories.title: Football clubs urged to ease police cost burden on taxpayers
stories.summary: Taxpayers in England and Wales shouldered £70 million of police funding for football matches last season, with incidents reported at 1,644 matches, the highest figure since 2017-18. Football clubs are being urged to contribute more to match policing costs, with Chief Constable Mark Roberts saying the current model "simply cannot continue". The UK Football Policing Unit is calling for a change to ensure clubs pay their fair share of policing expenses outside stadiums.

stories.title: Wildfires and record dry conditions trigger drought in parts of Wales
stories.summary: A drought has been declared in parts of north and mid Wales due to record dry conditions and wildfires, with exceptionally low river and groundwater levels, dry soils, and impacts on wildlife, farming, and forestry. The drought affects north and parts of mid Wales, with the rest of mid and south Wales in "prolonged dry weather" status. July is on course to become the driest in Wales for almost 190 years, with significant rainfall needed before the end of the month to avoid this record.

stories.title: Senior ministers and aides set to work from No 10 North
stories.summary: Senior ministers Lucy Powell and Angela Rayner will regularly work from No 10 North in Manchester, joining Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster Louise Haigh. The new Downing Street branch, currently based at Heron House, aims to employ up to 300 staff within 18 months. Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham plans to start working there at least once a week in the coming days.

stories.title: Saudis must recognise Israel for nuclear deal, says Trump
stories.summary: Former US President Trump has stated that Saudi Arabia must recognize Israel in order to join a nuclear deal, a condition that could pave the way for the kingdom's historic normalization of relations with Israel. This move would follow in the footsteps of the UAE, Bahrain, Sudan, Morocco, and Kazakhstan, which have already signed the Abraham Accords. If implemented, it could mark a significant leap forward for peace in the Middle East.

stories.title: Alonso optimistic about Aston Martin upgrade - will it decide his future?
stories.summary: Fernando Alonso is optimistic about Aston Martin's major upgrade at the Hungarian Grand Prix, which features a new chassis and significant aerodynamic changes, aiming to improve the team's performance. The upgrade is a result of Aston Martin's decision to introduce all new parts at once, rather than gradually, to make the most efficient use of their budget within the sport's spending cap. This upgrade comes as Alonso and Aston Martin look to unlock the car's performance and potentially turn their season around.

stories.title: Arsenal sign Greece forward Tzolis for £34m
stories.summary: Arsenal have signed Greece international Christos Tzolis from Club Brugge for £34m on a long-term contract, bringing the 24-year-old's exceptional technical ability and goal-scoring record to the Premier League champions. Tzolis scored 43 goals and provided 45 assists in 108 appearances for Club Brugge, and will join the team after the departure of Leandro Trossard to Besiktas. The signing is expected to raise the technical level of Arsenal's squad and bring positive energy to the team.

stories.title: Burnham says 20% business rates cut for English pubs 'a first step'
stories.summary: Andy Burnham announced a 20% cut to business rates for English pubs, clubs and live music venues from April, saving firms around £1,100 each. The £100m plan, funded by reviewing tax relief on vape shops, will benefit nearly 32,000 venues but excludes the largest live music venues. Details on eligibility will be revealed at the autumn Budget.

stories.title: Variety and CNN Expand Programming Partnership to Deliver Exclusive Awards Season Coverage
stories.summary: Variety and CNN have expanded their programming partnership to deliver exclusive awards season coverage, including the development, production, and distribution of premium content. The partnership builds on their previous collaborations, such as the Actors on Actors series and a town hall with Timothée Chalamet and Matthew McConaughey. This expanded partnership will provide in-depth coverage of the awards season.

stories.title: Music Industry Moves: Yung Miami Signs With WME; City Parks Foundation to Honor Joe Killian and Alex Blavatnik
stories.summary: Rapper Yung Miami has signed with WME for worldwide representation as she prepares her solo debut on Uptown Records. Her single “Spend Dat” hit No. 17 on the Hot 100 after topping Billboard’s Rap Airplay chart. Meanwhile, New York’s City Parks Foundation will honor Joe Killian and Alex Blavatnik at its 2026 benefit in Central Park’s Rumsey Playfield on September 10.

stories.title: How to Launder Money But Not Like Me, by George Cottrell (as imagined by John Crace)
stories.summary: George Cottrell was arrested in 2018 at Chicago O’Hare Airport for conspiring to launder money and served eight months in prison after a plea deal reduced charges to wire fraud. Authorities say just 0.05% of illicit global funds are intercepted by anti-money-laundering laws, leaving 5% of the world economy untouched. Cottrell now claims his book aims to expose gaps in enforcement rather than enable crime.

stories.title: Burnham pauses early prison release scheme
stories.summary: Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham has paused a controversial early prison release scheme set to begin this September. The plan would have freed inmates after serving half their sentences instead of two-thirds, easing prison overcrowding. Burnham now orders an urgent review to assess public safety risks before any releases proceed.

stories.title: ‘Weird Al’ Yankovic Turned Down Million-Dollar Offer to Perform in Saudi Arabia: ‘I Don’t Need to Take Any Money if It Makes Me Feel Icky’
stories.summary: Comedian "Weird Al" Yankovic revealed he declined a seven-figure offer to perform at Saudi Arabia's Riyadh Comedy Festival last fall. The satirist told TribLive he turned down the deal despite never receiving such a high payment for a single show. Yankovic joins other top comics who accepted the controversial event's invitations.

stories.title: Yemen's Houthis attack Saudi tanker as US launches more Iran strikes
stories.summary: Yemen’s Houthi rebels struck two Saudi-flagged oil tankers in the Red Sea late Wednesday, igniting a fire on one vessel. The U.S. launched its 12th straight night of strikes on Iran, while Saudi Arabia confirmed one tanker was hit. Oil prices surged to $100 per barrel, the highest since May, as regional tensions escalated.

stories.title: Lawmakers prepare bill requiring AI ‘kill switch’
stories.summary: Lawmakers are set to introduce the "AI Kill Switch Act" on Thursday, requiring AI companies to shut down or throttle their systems on orders from the Department of Homeland Security in "loss-of-control" scenarios involving at least 10 deaths or $100 million in economic damages. The bill would also mandate AI companies to build and implement emergency shutdown technology and report safety incidents. Violations of these orders could result in fines of up to $20 million per day.

stories.title: Risk of Parkinson’s disease may increase with prolonged exposure to road traffic noise
stories.summary: A new study has found that prolonged exposure to road traffic noise may increase the risk of Parkinson's disease by 3% for every 11.5 decibel rise in noise level, with the study period spanning 18 years and involving 3.1 million Danish participants aged 40 and over. The research suggests that having a quiet part of the home may mitigate this association. The study's findings were published in Jama Neurology.

stories.title: Google fined €890m by EU for favouring its own apps over rivals
stories.summary: Google has been fined €890 million by the European Union for breaching the Digital Markets Act by favouring its own apps over rivals. The fine follows two separate breaches, including a €460 million penalty for promoting its services in search results. Google disputes the ruling, warning it will reduce key features for European users.

stories.title: Ghanaians' ICC petition over migrant attacks 'opportunistic', says South Africa
stories.summary: South Africa has dismissed a petition filed at the International Criminal Court by two Ghanaians, calling it opportunistic and lacking the legal threshold to warrant an investigation. The petition, submitted last week, accuses the South African government of failing to prevent and punish attacks on foreigners, which could amount to crimes against humanity. Diplomatic tensions between Ghana and South Africa continue to rise as Ghana has claimed one of its citizens was killed in related violence.

stories.title: France evacuates 20,000 as wildfires spread in south-west
stories.summary: France evacuated 20,000 people in the Gironde region on July 16 as wildfires scorched 3,100 hectares near the Bay of Arcachon. Authorities moved 8,800 more residents and visitors as flames spread, with no injuries reported. Firefighters warned worsening conditions as temperatures and winds intensified overnight.

stories.title: Global Oil Price Hits $100 a Barrel for First Time Since May
stories.summary: The recent rebound reflects traders’ growing concern about the expanding war, which now extends from the Persian Gulf to the Red Sea.

stories.title: Ford Will Use Apple Maps in New Self-Driving System
stories.summary: In a first, Apple software will influence the way a new line of electric vehicles made by Ford Motor operates, and other automakers may follow.

stories.title: For South Korea, U.S.-Saudi Nuclear Deal Exposes a Double Standard
stories.summary: President Trump has put Saudi Arabia on the path to enriching uranium on its own soil, a demand South Korea has been making for years.

stories.title: A Peptide Showdown: F.D.A. Scientists May Clash With R.F.K.’s Agenda
stories.summary: Agency scientists are advising against allowing widespread use of several peptide injections. Robert F. Kennedy Jr., the health secretary, has said people should be free to experiment.

stories.title: Poorest children in England 40% more likely to be hospitalised for lung infections
stories.summary: Children in England’s poorest areas are 40% more likely to be hospitalised for lung infections than those in wealthier regions, new NHS data shows. In 2024-25, 1,941 per 100,000 children in the most deprived areas were admitted compared to 1,380 per 100,000 in the least deprived. Experts warn air pollution and deprivation likely drive the gap, with calls for urgent action.

stories.title: ‘Why do I feel crappy?’: women are learning about their luteal phases - and Maha is weaponizing it
stories.summary: A growing number of women are tracking their luteal phases—those 14 days between ovulation and menstruation—after health coach Emily Rae Detrick highlighted its emotional and physical tolls. Detrick, who promotes the approach through the "Make America Healthy Again" movement, argues the phase’s hormonal shifts can trigger irritability and cravings if pregnancy doesn’t occur. Experts say awareness of these cycles can help manage symptoms, though some warn against oversimplifying complex biological processes.

Mixon missed the entire 2025 season due to the injury and was released by the Texans in March. His decision comes after a successful career, including four 1,000-yard rushing seasons and two Pro Bowl nods.
